verdict: High risk of AI displacement in routine tasks, but human expertise is essential for complex decision-making and high-stakes procedures.

reasoning_steps.title: Task Automation in Surgery
reasoning_steps.body: Surgery involves complex decision-making and high-stakes procedures, which are less likely to be automated by AI.

reasoning_steps.title: Licensure and Regulation
reasoning_steps.body: Surgeons must meet strict licensure requirements and adhere to regulatory guidelines, creating a moat that protects their role from AI displacement.

reasoning_steps.title: Physical Dexterity and Embodied Work
reasoning_steps.body: Surgery requires precise physical movements and interaction with patients, making it difficult for AI systems to replicate without significant advancements in robotics and haptics.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Summary of surgeon role and responsibilities

adjacent_careers.name: Anesthesiologist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both surgeons and anesthesiologists work closely together in high-stakes procedures, making this role adjacent to surgery.

adjacent_careers.name: Orthopedic Surgeon
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Orthopedic surgeons specialize in surgical procedures related to musculoskeletal systems, making them closely related to general surgeons.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered surgical planning tool gains FDA clearance
recent_developments.impact: This development raises concerns about the potential for AI to displace human surgeons in routine tasks, but its impact on high-stakes procedures is unclear.

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
